articles.title: The Zod trap: Why your AI agent is breaking MCPFusion architecture
articles.summary: I was reviewing an agent's recent output for a new MCP server implementation, and at first glance, it looked perfect. articles.title: Using AlterLab as an MCP Tool to Feed Live Web Data into AI Agent Workflows
articles.summary: TL;DR Use AlterLab as a Model Context Protocol tool to provide live web data to AI agents. The API handles anti-bot measures, proxies, and output formatting, letting your agent focus on reasoning rather than scraping mechanics. articles.author: AlterLab

articles.title: I Built 3 MCP Servers for AI Agents — Here's How They Work
articles.summary: What are MCP Servers? The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that lets AI agents use external tools through a unified interface. Think of it as USB-C for AI — one protocol connects any AI client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code with Cline) to any tool or data source. I built three...
articles.author: Kenneth Doster

articles.title: 🔌 I Tried 100 MCP Servers. These Are The Only 12 Worth Installing.
articles.summary: The Model Context Protocol ecosystem exploded to nearly 20,000 servers. Most are noise. I installed, wired up, and stress-tested 100 of them — mostly inside Claude Code — to find the handful that actually earn a permanent slot in your config. articles.title: MCP for Beginners: Why Every AI Engineer and Developer Should Learn the Model Context Protocol
articles.summary: If you have spent any time building with large language models in the last year, you have hit the same wall everyone hits: your model is brilliant at reasoning but blind to the real world. articles.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced
articles.summary: Why put API Management in front of your MCP servers The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has quickly become the standard way for AI agents, such as GitHub Copilot in VS Code, to reach external tools and data. articles.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
articles.summary: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
